differences: The refactor now implements the approved idle/active/expanded contract, but the exact top-edge blend against a physical notch still lacks a fresh display screenshot.
The pet sprites and motion are custom reconstructions, so cadence and silhouette may still differ from the Mac Pet references.
Package-level tests were added in-plan but could not run in this CLI environment because neither XCTest nor Testing modules are exposed by the installed command-line toolchain.
suggestions: Run the app on a notched MacBook and capture a fresh screenshot of idle and active states to tune the notch-edge blend and width tolerances.
Compare a short screen recording against the Mac Pet walking and sleeping GIFs and adjust sprite scale, patrol range, and idle perch height as needed.
If package tests are required in this environment, install a full Xcode toolchain or an SDK/toolchain pair that exposes XCTest.
reasoning: The implementation now matches the planned state model and passed `swift build`, `./build.sh`, and a short runtime launch. The remaining gaps are visual capture on real hardware and package-test support in the current CLI toolchain.
